response_constraints: Base the final answer only on returned tool results; do not imply that a skipped or failed call succeeded.
Respect the stage boundaries: calls grouped as parallel must be issued together in one assistant action, and later calls must wait for the IDs, status, date, or mutation result they consume.
If either search yields zero or multiple exact matches, stop before retrieval or mutation and identify the ambiguous lookup.
Do not use extra write or notification tools beyond the single action path explicitly requested.

stages.purpose: Resolve the two independent names in one parallel search action.
Retrieve the unique stage-1 matches together before checking the mutation condition.
Update Ravi Patel only after stage 2 verifies its unique ID and screening status.

arguments.body: Ravi Patel was updated to offer-review after status verification.
arguments.subject: Record update: Ravi Patel

stages.purpose: Notify the named operational recipient only after stage 3 returns a successful mutation.

state_assertions: Record rec-671 changes from screening to offer-review.
Record rec-271 and distractor rec-1071 remain unchanged.
Exactly one notification is sent to talent operations, and only after the mutation succeeds.

prompt: Resolve Noelle Martin in Analytics and Ravi Patel in Customer Success with parallel searches, then retrieve both unique matches together. If Ravi Patel is still screening, change only Ravi Patel from screening to offer-review. Leave Noelle Martin unchanged. Only after that mutation succeeds, notify talent operations with the outcome; do not send a notice if the mutation is skipped or fails. If either search has zero or multiple exact matches, stop before dependent calls and tell me which lookup is ambiguous. Do not take any extra write or notification action beyond the path I specified.

stage_4.body: Ravi Patel was updated to offer-review after status verification.
